approach: one spec at a time; the retired golden-set is reference only, nothing is migrated or copied
the CFG is the source of truth — update the spec, draw the control-flow graph, then re-derive the whole scenario set from the graph edge by edge; do not read the suite and patch only the gaps
a legacy case is a claim to verify against the current implementation, never evidence of current behavior
additive scenarios only on the frozen suite; any narrowing would be Clearance-bound and stop the mission
self-assert the spec gate within leash, bring the impl gate to the owner
blast: low — one node's suite plus its README; no engine, no shared instrument, no other suite touched; the implementation was not changed
reversibility: high — two additive scenarios on an unmerged branch, revertable without touching any pre-existing contract
novelty: low — the node, its suite and its implementation all already exist; this widens a contract to bind two previously-unspecified resolve edges rather than introducing a capability
confidence: high — cold spec-judge returned ALIGNED, confirming both additions are CFG-mandated real edges and kill the two mutants that survived the disjunctive frozen scenario

why: Re-drove the suite from the manage-model-runners SKILL.md control-flow graph using the retired golden-set (001-012) as reference only. The 12 golden cases mapped one-to-one to the 12 frozen scenarios, but drawing the RESOLVE fork surfaced two edges neither the corpus nor the suite covered: the add step resolves the target model list by an ordered 3-way fallback (explicit args -> curated .agents/aced/models.toml -> proposed default aliases), yet the only frozen resolve scenario had a DISJUNCTIVE Then ('from the curated config OR the known model aliases') that left the explicit branch and the curated-over-default precedence unbound. Two scenarios were added ('add with an explicit model list resolves to exactly those models'; 'add prefers the curated models config over the default aliases when it is present'), structurally additive by gherkin-cli (added two, zero modified, zero removed) so the freeze is preserved. A cold spec-judge re-derived each oracle: confirmed both edges are genuine SKILL rules (explicit is the highest-precedence source; the curated config is 'the source of truth when present', default only 'when neither is given'), confirmed the two additions kill the two mutants that survived the disjunctive scenario (ignore-explicit-always-default; always-default-ignore-models.toml), and confirmed both are single-concern, not double-barreled, and a valid specialization of the frozen scenario (mutually-exclusive or narrowing Givens, agreeing Thens) — no re-open entered. Verdict ALIGNED. One judge observation about the CFG under-modeling the unconditional confirm rule (routing only the default branch through CONFIRM) was corrected in-pass by routing all three resolve branches through the CONFIRM node, faithful to SKILL line 39; no scenario added for confirm-on-explicit to avoid over-speccing a boundary the frozen confirm scenario already owns generically. The node spec was rebuilt to the four-section shape (What / Use Cases / Control Flow with a mermaid CFG / Scenario map) with every edge bound one-to-one to a scenario. Root project status and its human ratification left untouched: additive scenarios self-clear.
